question_statement: In GBTLA, when does assessment take place?
options: Only at the end of the lesson
hints: In GBTLA, assessment is not limited to the end of the lesson. Limiting assessment to the end prevents teachers from identifying learning gaps during the lesson.
Assessment may begin early, but it is not restricted to the beginning. It continues throughout the lesson.
GBTLA integrates assessment into the entire learning process, during activities, through feedback, and at the end, allowing continuous monitoring of learning.
Tests are only one form of assessment. GBTLA emphasizes ongoing observation and feedback, not just formal testing.

question_statement: During a game activity, a teacher listens to student discussions and observes how they solve problems. This is an example of:
hints: Summative assessment occurs after learning, not during the activity.
Observing students during learning helps the teacher understand thinking and provide support — this is formative assessment.
Final evaluation happens at the end, not during the activity.
Testing is structured assessment, while this is observation-based assessment.

question_statement: A student selects a wrong answer in a game and the game slows down their progress. What is this an example of?
hints: The purpose is not to punish, but to guide learning.
The feedback is immediate, not delayed.
The game provides instant feedback, allowing the student to recognize mistakes and adjust thinking immediately.
This is not just assessment — it actively supports learning.

question_statement: What is the main purpose of using Google Form quizzes in GBTLA?
options: To evaluate student understanding after the lesson
hints: Google Forms support assessment but do not replace teaching.
These quizzes are not designed for entertainment, but for evaluation.
Google Forms are used as summative assessment tools to check whether students have achieved learning outcomes.
While they may be completed at home or class, their purpose is evaluation, not workload.

question_statement: Which of the following BEST describes formative assessment?
options: It happens only after the lesson
It happens during learning and guides instruction
hints: That describes summative assessment.
Measuring final performance is not formative assessment.
Formative assessment happens during the lesson and helps the teacher adjust instruction based on student understanding.
It supports teaching, not replaces it.

question_statement: A teacher notices student making repeated mistakes during a game and immediately guides them. What is the main benefit of this approach?
hints: Time-saving is not the main objective here.
Discipline may improve, but that is not the core purpose.
Immediate guidance helps students correct mistakes during learning, which strengthens understanding.
This approach may actually require more active teaching.

question_statement: Which of the following are levels of assessment in GBTLA? (Select all that apply)
hints: Teachers observe and assess students during activities.
Games provide immediate feedback that supports assessment.
End-of-lesson quizzes help evaluate outcomes.
Homework alone does not represent the GBTLA assessment structure.
Final exams are not the primary focus in GBTLA.

question_statement: How do games provide effective feedback? (Select all that apply)
options: By showing correct or incorrect responses immediately
By allowing students to adjust their answers
By delaying results until the end
By slowing progress when answers are incorrect
By removing students from the activity
hints: Immediate response helps students recognize correctness instantly.
Students can rethink and improve their answers.
Delayed feedback reduces learning effectiveness.
Slowing progress signals incorrect understanding.
Students are not removed; they are supported to improve.

question_statement: Which statements describe the use of Google Forms in GBTLA? (Select all that apply)
options: They are linked to lesson plans on LMS
Teachers must create them from scratch
They provide quick assessment of understanding
They are only used for homework
hints: Forms are provided within LMS for each lesson.
Teachers are not required to create them independently.
They help quickly assess learning outcomes.
Responses are automatically collected and stored.
They are used for assessment, not limited to homework.

question_statement: A teacher is implementing assessment effectively in a GBTLA lesson. Which actions are appropriate? (Select all that apply)
options: Using game feedback to identify mistakes
Waiting until the end to assess learning
Using Google Form quizzes after the lesson
Attentively listen to the student responses during activities
hints: Observation is key to formative assessment.
Game feedback helps identify misconceptions.
Assessment should not be delayed until the end.
Summative assessment confirms learning outcomes.
Student responses provides the learning insight.
